title: SolarBench tests whether AI agents can run an industrial operations desk
friendlyTitle: A new test asks AI to manage solar farms for a full week
summary: SolarBench evaluates agents in week-long simulations of a remote solar-operations desk, including telemetry, alarms, work orders, parts and stakeholder email. Across 880 launch runs, even the strongest model passed only 53.8% of complete weeks, and repeated reliability fell sharply.
friendlySummary: SolarBench gives AI agents realistic solar-farm problems involving alarms, repairs, parts and emails. In its first tests, even the best model completed only about half of the weeks correctly.
whyItMatters: The benchmark measures long-horizon operational judgment rather than isolated answers. Businesses considering autonomous agents should test follow-through, cost, consistency and recovery across full workflows before trusting a strong one-off demo.
friendlyWhyItMatters: A good single answer does not prove that an AI can manage real work for days. Test whether it remembers tasks, controls costs and gives reliable results over time.
